beats.narration_text: Ahoj. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of binary counting solves towers of hanoi. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of binary counting solves towers of hanoi
output: ✓ Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi

beats.narration_text: Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. the core behaviour of binary counting solves towers of hanoi. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: speed-slider. Verify: the key behaviour of binary counting solves towers of hanoi. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: speed-slider. Verify: the key be

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from yt-hanoi-binary.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: binary counting solves towers of hanoi. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0.5 to 4. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0.5 to 4

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0.5 to 4

beats.narration_text: the prediction holds — binary counting solves towers of hanoi behaves as the equations say. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of binary counting solves towers of hanoi. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Binary Counting Solves Towers of Hanoi simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
